title: Silent parse failure in `mapMonthlyReportRow` / `mapWeeklyReportRow` (both services)
body_excerpt:
**1. Silent parse failure in `mapMonthlyReportRow` / `mapWeeklyReportRow` (both services)**

Switching from `.parse()` (throws) to `safeParse()` (returns `null`) on the detail mappers creates a silent
evidence: apps/api/src/services/monthly-report.ts:240-246 and weekly-report.ts:143-149 have `logger.warn` on parse failure, partially addressing 'no logging'. However the null is still passed to callers (monthly-report.ts:332, 350; weekly-report.ts:231, 249) which return it as `null`, and routes/progress.ts:147,167 convert both a genuine missing row and a schema-validation failure to `notFound(c, 'Report not found')` — HTTP 404. The discriminated null vs 404 issue is UNADDRESSED. No Sentry capture, no 500 for parse failure.
suggested_notion_title: API: parse failure in mapMonthlyReportRow/mapWeeklyReportRow silently returns 404
suggested_notion_body: Both `mapMonthlyReportRow` and `mapWeeklyReportRow` use `safeParse()` and return `null` on schema validation failure. Their callers pass `null` through to route handlers which call `notFound(c, 'Report not found')` — indistinguishable from a genuinely missing row. A DB row that fails Zod validation silently returns HTTP 404 rather than a 500 that would trigger Sentry. Fix: throw on parse failure (returns 500 + Sentry capture) or use a discriminated union so callers can distinguish parse failure from missing row. Files: apps/api/src/services/monthly-report.ts, apps/api/src/services/weekly-report.ts, apps/api/src/routes/progress.ts.

title: Fragile dark-mode detection in `practice/index.tsx`
body_excerpt:
**2. Fragile dark-mode detection in `practice/index.tsx`**

`const isDark = theme.background !== '#faf5ef'` compares against a magic hex value. If the light-mode background token changes, dark-mode det
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/practice/index.tsx:46 now uses `const isDark = colorScheme === 'dark'` via `useTheme()`. The magic hex `#faf5ef` is entirely absent from the file. All color references in usePracticeColors() use semantic theme tokens (e.g. `theme.practiceReviewBg`, `theme.practiceMint`) — no hardcoded hex values remain.

title: resolveTabShape` returns `'guardian'` during proxy sessions
body_excerpt:
**3. `resolveTabShape` returns `'guardian'` during proxy sessions**

When `isParentProxy: true`, the layout renders the 5-tab guardian shape, which includes the **Own Learning** tab. That tab shows the
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_layout.tsx:103 now returns `'learner'` when `isParentProxy` is true: `if (isParentProxy) return 'learner';`. This is evaluated before the isGuardianProfile check, so proxy sessions always get 4-tab learner shape with no Own Learning tab. The fix matches the CLAUDE.md tab-shape table.

title: Issue 1: mapMonthlyReportRow and mapWeeklyReportRow: schema-validation failure silently returns 404.
body_excerpt:
Issue 1: mapMonthlyReportRow and mapWeeklyReportRow: schema-validation failure silently returns 404.

Both functions now use safeParse and return null on failure. Their callers (getMonthlyReportForProfil
evidence: Same as finding comment-4425071308 Critical above. monthly-report.ts:332,350 and weekly-report.ts:231,249 still pass null through. routes/progress.ts:147,167 convert null to `notFound(c, 'Report not found')`. Schema validation failure and missing row are indistinguishable at the HTTP layer. `logger.warn` is present but no Sentry capture and no 500.

title: Issue 2: progress/weekly-report/_layout.tsx is missing unstable_settings.
body_excerpt:
Issue 2: progress/weekly-report/_layout.tsx is missing unstable_settings.

The layout contains both index.tsx and [weeklyReportId].tsx. CLAUDE.md requires any new nested Expo Router layout that contains
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress/weekly-report/_layout.tsx:4-6 exports `export const unstable_settings = { initialRouteName: 'index' };`. Both the export and the `initialRouteName: 'index'` prop on the Stack are present.

title: resolveTabShape(null) returns 'guardian' — wrong fail-safe direction
body_excerpt:
1. **resolveTabShape(null) returns 'guardian' — wrong fail-safe direction** (inline on _layout.tsx:94)
   When activeProfile is null (loading state), the function returns 'guardian' (5 tabs). This briefl
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_layout.tsx:102 still returns `'guardian'` when `activeProfile` is null: `if (!activeProfile) return 'guardian';`. The test at _layout.test.tsx:821-829 explicitly asserts this behavior (`expect(...).toBe('guardian')`) and has not been changed. Minimum-privilege would return 'learner' during loading state to avoid briefly showing the Own Learning tab to non-guardian users.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: resolveTabShape(null) returns 'guardian' — wrong fail-safe during profile load
suggested_notion_body: When `activeProfile` is null (profile loading state), `resolveTabShape` returns `'guardian'` (5 tabs including Own Learning). For the majority of users who are learners, this briefly flashes the Own Learning tab before hiding it. Minimum-privilege design would return `'learner'` while profile is unknown. Fix: change line 102 of apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_layout.tsx to `if (!activeProfile) return 'learner';` and update the corresponding test assertion.

title: mapMonthlyReportRow / mapWeeklyReportRow null conflated with 404
body_excerpt:
2. **mapMonthlyReportRow / mapWeeklyReportRow null conflated with 404** (inline on monthly-report.ts:330)
   A row that exists in the DB but fails Zod validation returns null, which the route handler conv
evidence: Duplicate of finding comment-4425071308 Critical. routes/progress.ts:147,167 still map null (from either missing row or parse failure) to `notFound`. No discriminated union introduced. The logger.warn added is the only partial mitigation.

title: ../../../.. <- wrong (integration-mock-guard REPO_ROOT depth from services/llm/)
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/api/src/services/llm/` (actual)
Line: `../../../../..`
Issue: `../../../..` <- **wrong**
evidence: The file has been moved from `apps/api/src/services/llm/integration-mock-guard.test.ts` to `apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts`. The current REPO_ROOT at line 26 is `resolve(__dirname, '../../../..')` — 4 levels up from `test-utils/` which correctly resolves to the repo root. The old location no longer exists.

body_excerpt:

Line: `../../../..`
Issue: `../../../..`
evidence: apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts:26 uses `resolve(__dirname, '../../../..')`. From `apps/api/src/test-utils/` that is: `..`=src, `../..`=api, `../../..`=apps, `../../../..`=repo root. Depth is correct for the new location.

evidence: The file move is now complete: `apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts` exists and the old path `apps/api/src/services/llm/integration-mock-guard.test.ts` does not. The plan doc at docs/plans/2026-05-12-shared-test-utility-framework-plan.md:229 marks it 'Done 2026-05-14' and the verification command references the new correct path.

title: The detection regex is: (jest.doMock not caught by guard)
body_excerpt:
The detection regex is:

```ts
/jest\.mock\(\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]]/g
```

This matches `jest.mock(` but not `jest.doMock(`. If a contributor uses `jest.doMock` in an `*.integration.test.ts` file, it byp
evidence: apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts:94 still uses `/jest\.mock\(\s*['"]([ ^'"]+)['"]]/g` — no `(?:do)?` extension. No `jest.doMock` calls exist in any current `*.integration.test.ts` file (grep confirms zero hits), so the gap is latent. The feedback-delivery-failed.test.ts uses `jest.doMock` at line 432 but it is a regular `.test.ts`, not `.integration.test.ts`. Risk is real but dormant.
suggested_notion_title: API: integration-mock-guard regex misses jest.doMock — bypass possible
suggested_notion_body: The guard in `apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts` uses `/jest\.mock\(/g` which does not catch `jest.doMock(`. A contributor who reads the docstring permitting Inngest transport capture might write `jest.doMock('../client', ...)` in an `*.integration.test.ts` and bypass the guard entirely. Fix: extend regex to `/jest\.(?:do)?[Mm]ock\(/g` or add a second pass for `doMock`.

title: Guard header says Inngest transport capture is allowed but it is not in ALLOWED_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_MOCKS
body_excerpt: The guard header (lines 6-9) says integration tests may mock `Inngest transport capture` as a true external boundary. But `ALLOWED_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_MOCKS` only contains `services/sentry` and `service
evidence: apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts:8-9 docstring: 'only true external boundaries (Stripe, Sentry, Clerk JWKS, email providers, push notifications, LLM providers via the registry, Inngest transport capture)'. But ALLOWED_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_MOCKS at lines 21-24 only contains `services/sentry` and `services/stripe` — Inngest transport (`../inngest/client`) is NOT listed. Line 141-142 self-test confirms `../../inngest/client` IS flagged as internal. The docstring promise and the allowlist are mismatched, creating a documentation trap for contributors.
suggested_notion_title: API: integration-mock-guard docstring says Inngest transport is allowed but it is not in the allowlist
suggested_notion_body: The guard header comment (apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts:8-9) lists 'Inngest transport capture' as a permitted boundary mock for integration tests, but `ALLOWED_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_MOCKS` only contains `services/sentry` and `services/stripe`. A contributor reading the docstring permission would write `jest.mock('../inngest/client', ...)` and find the guard flags it anyway. Fix: either add `inngest/client` to `ALLOWED_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_MOCKS` (with a regex matching the transport capture pattern), or remove Inngest from the docstring permit list.

title: Nothing technically HIGH - no production code changed, no security surface modified.
body_excerpt:
Nothing technically HIGH - no production code changed, no security surface modified.

---
evidence: This CCR finding is a reviewer meta-comment asserting no High severity issues exist in PR#262. No action needed — the PR only touches test files and audit docs. Treating as ADDRESSED (non-issue).

title: Lost explanatory comment in `billing.test.ts`
body_excerpt:
**1. Lost explanatory comment in `billing.test.ts`** (inline comment posted)

The conversion of `jest.mock('../services/billing', ...)` deleted the comment:
```
// Use real ProfileRemovalNotImplementedErr
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/billing.test.ts:113-114 contains: `// Use real ProfileRemovalNotImplementedError so instanceof checks in the // route handler match production behaviour.` The comment was not lost — it is present inside the mock factory body.

title: Selective mock semantics changed in `consent-revocation.test.ts`
body_excerpt:
**2. Selective mock semantics changed in `consent-revocation.test.ts`** (inline comment posted)

The old `services/consent` mock was intentionally explicit: it only pulled `calculateAge` from the real mo
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/consent-revocation.test.ts:47-58 spreads `...actual` then overrides 4 DB-backed functions. Pattern A (opt-out) is applied. The gc1-allow comment says 'calculateAge kept real' but does not list all non-overridden exports. If the SUT gains a new call to a non-listed DB function, it will silently run the real implementation against an absent test DB rather than fail fast with 'undefined is not a function'. The original opt-in semantics provided safer failure detection.
suggested_notion_title: API: consent-revocation.test.ts opt-out mock semantics risk silent real-DB calls
suggested_notion_body: Pattern A conversion of consent-revocation.test.ts changed from opt-in (only calculateAge real) to opt-out (all exports real, 4 overridden). If the SUT calls any non-overridden DB-backed function (e.g. revokeConsent, createPendingConsent), it runs against the absent test DB rather than failing fast. The original selective mock was intentionally narrow. Consider keeping the opt-in shape for services with many DB-backed exports to preserve fast-failure semantics.

body_excerpt:
**3. Planning document committed to `plans/`** (inline comment posted)

`plans/proud-roaming-wigderson.md` describes how to rebase this PR onto main using `-X theirs` and is specific to a conflict-resol
evidence: `plans/proud-roaming-wigderson.md` does not exist in the repository. The `plans/` directory contains 6 other files but not this one. It was deleted before or after merge.

title: gc1-allow reason uniformly erased (spans entire PR)
body_excerpt:
**1. gc1-allow reason uniformly erased (spans entire PR)**

Every pre-existing annotation carried a reason explaining why the boundary needs a mock, such as "external-boundary - DB-dependent; calculateAg
evidence: Grep across apps/api/src confirms ALL 337 `gc1-allow: pattern-a conversion` annotations use identical generic text. No file has a `gc1-allow:` annotation with any other reason (0 matches for `gc1-allow: (?!pattern-a conversion)`). The original specific reasons (e.g. 'isolates unit test from DB-backed filing service', 'prevents real LLM calls') are gone. Future audit scripts relying on reason text to classify why mocks are retained will find only 'pattern-a conversion' uniformly.
suggested_notion_title: API: all gc1-allow annotations use generic 'pattern-a conversion' reason — specific rationale lost
suggested_notion_body: The mock cleanup PR replaced all specific gc1-allow reasons with the generic 'pattern-a conversion'. Original annotations explained: 'isolates unit test from DB-backed filing service', 'prevents real LLM calls while asserting topic generation boundary', 'calculateAge is real', etc. 337 annotations now carry identical boilerplate. ESLint only checks the annotation exists, not its quality — so this passes lint but loses the institutional knowledge the annotations were meant to carry. Consider restoring service-specific reasons for the highest-risk mocks.

title: Opt-in to opt-out mock semantics (multiple files)
body_excerpt:
**2. Opt-in to opt-out mock semantics (multiple files)**

Before this PR, many mocks were opt-in: the factory returned only the exports the test needed. If the SUT accidentally called a non-listed funct
evidence: Pattern A (`...actual` spread + targeted overrides) is applied codebase-wide across 104 files. For example, progress.test.ts:79-97 spreads all of `services/progress` and overrides 7 functions. Any new DB-backed function added to a service that is not manually added to the override list will silently run real code against absent test DB. This is a systemic semantic change affecting all converted tests — the opt-out pattern is the current state.

title: Stale exit metric in docs/audit/goal-spike-gc1-drain.md
body_excerpt:
**1. Stale exit metric in docs/audit/goal-spike-gc1-drain.md**

Metric 2 in that doc reads: "no new gc1-allow annotations added (baseline: 70)". The PR adds a gc1-allow: pattern-a conversion annotation
evidence: docs/audit/goal-spike-gc1-drain.md:30 still reads 'Current baseline: 288 internal mock calls across 92 API test files + 4 cross-package integration test files. 70 pre-existing gc1-allow annotations.' A 'Superseded draft' banner was added at lines 2-5, but the stale metric is not corrected or struck through. The banner points to goal-spike-mock-claude.md but does not fix the misleading baseline number of 70.
suggested_notion_title: Docs: goal-spike-gc1-drain.md stale baseline metric (70 gc1-allow) not corrected after PR#262
suggested_notion_body: docs/audit/goal-spike-gc1-drain.md line 30 states 'baseline: 70 pre-existing gc1-allow annotations'. After PR#262, the count is 337+ across API alone. A 'Superseded' banner was added but the stale metric remains uncorrected. Audit tooling or future contributors reading the doc will get a false baseline. Fix: add a strikethrough or explicit correction note on line 30 with the post-PR#262 actual count.

title: Orphaned trailing annotation in filing-timed-out-observe.test.ts line 35
body_excerpt:
**2. Orphaned trailing annotation in filing-timed-out-observe.test.ts line 35**

After the multi-line expansion the closing line reads: }); // gc1-allow: inngest framework boundary — a leftover from the
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/filing-timed-out-observe.test.ts:35 is `});` with no trailing gc1-allow comment. Grep for 'gc1-allow.*inngest framework' across this file returns zero matches. The orphaned annotation was removed.
